title: Frontend Batch 38 - JSDOM Navigation Warning Isolation
summary: Isolated and removed the jsdom document navigation warning from the Vitest suite by correcting the auth CSRF logout failure test fixture.
testBugsFound: The logout failure test accidentally triggered the session-expired redirect path because the authenticated session payload was missing.
The test now reaches the intended logout endpoint failure and no longer causes jsdom navigation.

validation.command: npx skills find jsdom navigation vitest location testing
validation.notes: Results were general Vitest/frontend testing skills; no new skill was installed.
Final run: 1 file, 11 tests, no navigation warning.

validation.command: npm test -- --run src/test/api-base.test.ts src/test/api-client-no-cookie.test.ts src/test/auth-csrf.test.ts src/test/auth-login-url.test.ts
validation.notes: 4-file warning group passed with no navigation warning.

validation.command: npm test -- --run src/test/api-base.test.ts src/test/api-client-no-cookie.test.ts src/test/auth-csrf.test.ts src/test/auth-login-url.test.ts src/test/azure-backup-lib.test.ts src/test/blog-content.test.ts src/test/blog-detail-related.test.tsx src/test/blog-notion-workspace.test.tsx src/test/e2e-latency-budget.test.ts
validation.notes: 9-file warning group passed with no navigation warning.
Final run: 81 files, 559 tests; jsdom navigation warning did not recur.

remainingGaps: Pact V3 upgrade warnings remain noisy.
Full Playwright e2e still needs Docker Desktop WSL integration or an already running local stack.
Full Vitest remains slow because worker count is bounded for stability.
recommendation: Proceed to Frontend Batch 39 - Pact Warning and Contract Test Stability Reinforcement.
